## Source Description

WikiDocs page introducing a Korean practical guide to NousResearch's open-source AI agent framework Hermes Agent. The page states that the book was written with AI assistance and that some descriptions may contain errors despite review efforts.

## Main Topics From Table of Contents

- Hermes Agent introduction and comparison with other AI agents.
- Installation, system requirements, cloud API connection, quick start, settings, and updates.
- First conversation, CLI basics, model routing, slash commands, and voice mode.
- Tool system, terminal backend, practical tool use, and Codex App-Server runtime.
- Memory system: MEMORY.md, USER.md, session search, Honcho, and context compression.
- Personality and context: SOUL.md and AGENTS.md.
- Skill system: skill structure, automatic skill creation, bundled skills, Skills Hub, custom skills, and background curator.
- MCP integration, MCP client setup, extending capabilities through MCP, and exposing Hermes as an MCP server.
- Messaging gateways for Telegram, Discord, Microsoft Teams, and other platforms.
- Repeated and continuous execution through cron scheduling, automated reports, operational tuning, and persistent goals.
- Security: five-layer security model, container isolation, production checklist, command allowlist, multi-agent isolation, and profile separation.
- Architecture and advanced features: internal structure, self-evolution system with DSPy and GEPA, Kanban multi-agent collaboration, and local OAuth proxy.
- Practical projects: code analysis agent, daily news briefing automation, team collaboration bot, and Mac Mini home intelligence hub.
- Appendices on handoff from Claude Code to Hermes and local LLM usage.

## Page Claims To Treat Carefully

- The page says it is not a simple translation of official documentation, but a practical guide based on hands-on experience.
- The page says it compares local LLM and cloud API tradeoffs using actual tests.
- The page says it compares Hermes Agent with six AI agents including OpenClaw, NemoClaw, and Claude Code.
- The page says it includes local LLM benchmark comparison results.
- Because the page includes an AI-assisted writing notice, factual details should be checked against official Hermes Agent documentation or the GitHub repository before being used for decisions.
